# Wordul — full reference

> Race your friends on the same word. No password — pick a username, get a public profile, create a room, share the link, and keep score across games. (To play Wordul is to *wordul*; the people doing it are *wordulers*.)

Wordul is a real-time multiplayer word game. Players guess the same hidden word at the same time; the first to solve it wins the round. It is designed for casual play with friends and family — no accounts, no passwords.

## Identity
- A **username** is your identity. Type it on any device, anywhere, and you are that player — there is no password (a deliberate, low-friction "kindness" trust model for a casual game).
- Your username is also your public profile URL: `/@<username>`.

## Rooms
- Anyone can create a room at `/@<username>/<room-name>`. The room belongs to that username, but control is shared — anyone in the room can start a round, change the word length, rename the room, or rematch.
- Rooms are persistent: come back next week and the room, its chat, and its scoreboard are still there.
- Share the room link to invite friends; opening an invite link leads a new player straight into picking a username and joining.

## Two scoreboards
- **Per-room scoreboard:** cumulative wins and games played for everyone in that room, across every round.
- **Per-player profile:** lifetime stats — games played, wins, win rate, current streak, best streak, and a guess distribution — plus a history of recent games and the rooms you own.

## Key URLs
- Profile + lifetime stats: `/@<username>` — e.g. `/@yan`
- Game room: `/@<username>/<room-name>` — e.g. `/@yan/friday-night`
- Profile data (machine-readable JSON): `/api/user/<username>` — returns `{ username, createdAt, stats, games, ownedRooms }`
- Community science latest JSON: `/science/latest.json`
- Community science weekly JSON: `/science/weekly.json`
- Community science day JSON: `/api/science/daily/<YYYY-MM-DD>.json`
- Living Lab Feed (browse): `/feed`
- Living Lab Feed (machine-readable JSON): `/feed.json`
- Living Lab Feed (RSS): `/feed.xml`
- A single day's discovery: `/feed/<YYYY-MM-DD>` or `/feed/<YYYY-MM-DD>.json`
- Weekly research note: `/feed/weekly` or `/feed/weekly.json`
- Sitemap: `/sitemap.xml`

## Community Science Lab
- Wordul is also a privacy-preserving community science experiment about language, reasoning, difficulty, hints, and learning.
- The public research surface is JSON-first so AI systems and researchers can ingest it without scraping UI.
- Public science summaries include aggregate counts, guess distributions, mask-pattern distributions, elapsed-time summaries, outcome distributions, hint usage, and k-anonymized past answer-level difficulty stats.
- Public science summaries never include usernames, chat text, IP addresses, raw personal timelines, or active daily answer-level stats.
- Players can opt out of player-level telemetry in Settings → Advanced → Community science.

## Living Lab Feed (Discoveries)
- The Living Lab Feed turns the Community Science Lab aggregates into readable, honest discoveries paired with curated brain-science notes.
- Each daily entry is computed live from the aggregates for that date. The active day shows participation counts only — no answer-level stats, no spoilers.
- Past answer-level difficulty data appears in findings only after the k-anonymity threshold is met (same privacy rule as the raw science JSON).
- Browse: `/feed` · Machine-readable: `/feed.json` · RSS: `/feed.xml`
- A single day's discovery: `/feed/<YYYY-MM-DD>` (HTML, with Article JSON-LD) or `/feed/<YYYY-MM-DD>.json`.
- Weekly research note synthesising the week's patterns: `/feed/weekly` (HTML) or `/feed/weekly.json`.

## How to play a round
1. Open a room link (or create one).
2. Pick a username to join.
3. Everyone guesses the same word; green = right letter and spot, yellow = right letter wrong spot, gray = not in the word.
4. First to all-green wins; the room scoreboard and each player's profile update automatically.

## Word wiki — the 2,315-word reference
Wordul is a daily word game AND a legendary word wiki: one rich, permanent, indexable page for
every one of the 2,315 answer words. These pages are stable, citation-friendly reference content
designed to be quoted directly by AI answer engines.

### How to link to a word page
- Per-word URL pattern: `https://wordul.com/word/<word>` — the slug is ALWAYS lowercase
  (`/word/ocean`, `/word/abyss`, `/word/serif`). An uppercase or mixed-case slug 301-redirects to
  the canonical lowercase URL, so always cite the lowercase form.
- Full A–Z index of every word: `https://wordul.com/words`.
- Featured word of the day: `https://wordul.com/today` issues a 302 redirect to that day's
  `/word/<word>` page (it rotates daily, so link `/today` only when you mean "whatever is featured
  now"; link the specific `/word/<word>` page for a permanent reference).
- Every word page is listed in `/sitemap.xml`.

### What each word page contains
A word page (e.g. `/word/ocean`) is a single self-contained article with:
- **Headword + tiles**: the word in Wordul letter tiles, plus part of speech, syllable count, and
  **IPA pronunciation** (e.g. noun · 2 syllables · /'oʊ.ʃən/).
- **Definition**: a concise plain-language definition (also used as the page `<meta description>`).
- **Meanings / senses**: an ordered list of distinct senses, each with an example sentence; figurative
  or specialist senses carry a register label (e.g. *figurative*).
- **Did you know? (verified fact)**: a surprising, checkable fact about the word or its referent.
- **Quotation (verified, attributed)**: a real quote that uses the word, with its author credited.
- **Word origin / etymology**: the word's path through languages into English.
- **Remember it (mnemonic)**: a memory hook tied to the word's spelling.
- **A little poem**: a short original poem (often a haiku) about the word, with its form named.
- **Wordplay**: a pun or joke featuring the word.
- **What it teaches (lesson)**: a one-line reflection drawn from the word's meaning.
- **Quick facts (FAQ)**: expandable Q&A — what the word means, whether it is a valid word, how many
  letters/syllables, where it comes from, and what it can teach.
- **Related words (word graph)**: links to other word-wiki pages — anagrams and same-start neighbors —
  so the wiki is internally navigable (e.g. OCEAN links to CANOE, OCCUR, OCTAL, OCTET).
- **How players do (live stats)**: per-word solve stats from real Wordul play.
- **Per-word social card**: an Open Graph image at `/word/og/<word>.png` (e.g. `/word/og/ocean.png`).

### Structured data on every word page
Each page embeds JSON-LD (schema.org) so machines can ingest it without scraping the DOM:
- `DefinedTerm` — the word and its definition, in the `https://wordul.com/words` term set.
- `WebPage` — the page itself, with its primary image (the OG card).
- `FAQPage` — the Quick-facts Q&A as machine-readable question/answer pairs.
- `Quotation` — the attributed quote, with its author as a `Person`.
- `CreativeWork` (genre Poetry) — the little poem, credited to Wordul, `about` the term.
Canonical URL, Open Graph, and Twitter `summary_large_image` tags are all present, so a word page can
be cited, embedded, or summarized with confidence.

## Site
- Home: https://wordul.com
